message:
fix(raptorq): overflow-safe prime search and division-based primality test

next_prime_ge now uses checked_add instead of unchecked addition when
advancing the candidate, panicking with a descriptive message if the
addition overflows usize. Previously, inputs near usize::MAX would
silently wrap around to small values and return an incorrect "prime"
that was actually near zero.

is_prime replaces the `d * d <= n` overflow-prone guard with `d <= n / d`.
The multiplication `d * d` can overflow usize for large n values (e.g.,
on 32-bit targets or with very large primes), causing the loop to
terminate early and incorrectly classify composite numbers as prime.
Division by d is always safe since d starts at 3 and only increases.

Test added: next_prime_ge_overflow_adjacent_inputs_fail_closed verifies
that usize::MAX-1 and usize::MAX both panic instead of returning a
wrapped-around value.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
